mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction, forcing earnings downgrades and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production above 0.5 percent signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, driving short-term momentum and positive 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of recovery phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment below 4.0 percent indicates a soft-landing policy stance that supports equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of tightening cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation that triggers risk-off capital flows and negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5 percent is followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipated central bank tightening, generating negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL correlates with positive 5-day returns (Rank IC > 0.02) over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 in a single month signals strong labor demand that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200k yields negative 20-day returns in >60% of recovery phase observations.
